rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LI TIAN ZHANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The assessment fee does not appear to have been paid, however, as alleged in paragraph 18 of the petition. The services to the person were for slightly fewer than 7 years, but because the services to the estate were for slightly more than 7 years, the somewhat inaccurate allegations in connection with Conservator's fee request will be disregarded. The addresses for the persons entitled to notice were not set forth in the petition, so the Court can only assume that notice was properly served. (A proposed order should not include orders that were not in the petition prayer.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LAMBERT GENZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Grant Amended Petition For Return Of Assets
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Petitioner's Motion to Grant Amended Petition for (1) Return of Estate Assets as to Zi Yu Deng, Paul Yi Wei Deng, Ka Yu Ma, Jianming Yu, Jianjun Yu Filed by Steven Kmucha. Therein, Petitioner seeks an entry of default judgment on the ground that Respondents failed to timely respond. (Petitioner's Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support of Motion to Grant Amended Petition for (1) Return of Estate Assets as to Zi Yu Deng, Paul Yi Wei Deng, Ka Yu Ma, Jianming Yu, Jianjun Yuon, p. 2:11-12.) Petitioner's Motion to Grant Amended Petition for (1) Return of Estate Assets as to Zi Yu Deng, Paul Yi Wei Deng, Ka Yu Ma, Jianming Yu, Jianjun Yu is DENIED. Default judgment may be entered where a defendant fails to answer a complaint. (Code of Civil Proc. sec. 585(b).) Respondents have not failed to answer Petitioner's complaint; Respondents filed their Amended Response to Amended Petition to Return of Estate Assets filed by Steven Kmucha on June 7, 2024. Thereafter, on June 28, 2024, Petitioner filed the instant motion seeking entry of default judgment on the ground that Respondents' Amended Response was untimely. Pursuant to Prob. Code sec. 1043, an interested party may appear and make a response or objection in writing or orally at the hearing. When Respondents filed their Amended Response on June 7, 2024, the hearing on Petitioner's Amended Petition for (1) Return of Estate Assets from Qubao Yu, Chui F Yu, Bolin Yu, Zi Yu Deng, Paul Yi Wei Deng, Ka Yu Ma, Jianming Yu, Jianjun Yu; (2) for Invalidation of Deed; (3) for Undue Influence; (4) for Financial Elder Abuse; (5) Punitive Damages was scheduled for June 12, 2024. As such, Respondents' Amended Response was timely because it was filed prior to the scheduled hearing on the matter. Respondents shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OTIS R. DAMSLET
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/04/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 06/26/2024) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petition must be amended and renoticed to pray for a specific period (e.g., months) for which the administration of this estate is sought to be continued. See LRSF 14.35(J)(2). 4) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 5) Each status report must include all allegations required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1), even if they may have been included in previous reports. 6) The decedent died more than 2 years ago, but no first interim account covering the 2-year period from the date of death has been filed. See LRSF 14.34(A). If the beneficiaries waived the first account, please file signed waivers. Otherwise, the Court may require the first account. See section 12201(c)(1).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL ROBIN FLICK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/18/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) The copy of the deed attached to the petition shows title vested in Decedent's and Petitioner's names, as tenants in common, so it appears that not the entire property would pass to the petitioner. Please provide a courtesy copy of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/9/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
